prompt: List ALL the papers in this notebook with their: 1) Full title, 2) All authors, 3) Year published, 4) Key topic. Format as a bibliography. Include as many papers as possible.
response:
Here is a comprehensive bibliography of all the clearly identifiable papers presented in the provided notebook from the 10th Hydraulics Conference / 34th IAHR World Congress (2011). 

**1. Arias, M.E., Cochrane, T.A., Caruso, B., Killeen, T., & Kummu, M. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* A landscape approach to assess impacts of hydrological changes to vegetation communities of the Tonle Sap Floodplain [1].
* *Key Topic:* Floodplain hydrology and vegetation communities [1].

**2. Arias-Hidalgo, M., Villa-Cox, G., van Griensven, A., & Mynett, A.E. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Filling missing data in streamflow series for supporting models [2].
* *Key Topic:* Streamflow modeling and data imputation methods [2].

**3. Brasington, J., Rennie, C.D., Vericat, D., Williams, R., Goodsell, B., Hicks, M., & Batalla, R. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Monitoring Braided River Morphodynamics with an Acoustic Doppler Current Profiler [3].
* *Key Topic:* Braided river morphodynamics and acoustic tracking methods [3].

**4. Chen, H., Tkalich, P., & Malanotte-Rizzoli, P. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Data Analysis and Numerical Modeling of Sea Level Anomalies in the South China Sea [4].
* *Key Topic:* Sea level anomalies and hydrodynamic modeling [4].

**5. Gao, J. Z. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Newly Achievements on dam Hydraulic Research in China [5].
* *Key Topic:* Layout of hydraulic structures and energy dissipators in high dams [5].

**6. Ha, T., Lin, P., Jin, S., Seo, K.-H., & Cho, Y.-S. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Solitary Wave Run-up around a Conical Island with the Internal Wave Maker [6].
* *Key Topic:* Solitary wave run-up and 3D Navier-Stokes modeling [6], [7].

**7. Haddad, K., Rahman, A., Kuczera, G., & Micevski, T. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Regional Flood Frequency Analysis in New South Wales Using Bayesian GLS Regression: Comparison of Fixed Region and Region-of-influence Approaches [8].
* *Key Topic:* Regional flood frequency analysis and Bayesian regression [8].

**8. Hadler, J., & Broekel, K. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Cost-benefit analysis for low potential hydropower converters [9].
* *Key Topic:* Small hydropower, low head differences, and cost-benefit analysis [9].

**9. Jahra, F., Kawahara, Y., Hasegawa, F., & Yamamoto, T. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Prediction of Momentum and Solute Transfer in a Vegetated Compound Channel [10].
* *Key Topic:* Momentum transfer, solute transport, and emergent vegetation modeling [10].

**10. Jia, Y.W., Lei, X.H., Zhou, Z.H., Yang, G.Y., Qiu, Y.Q., & Niu, C.W. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Development and Application of a Decision-support System for Water Resources Distribution in the Wei River Basin [11].
* *Key Topic:* Water resources optimal distribution and Decision-Support Systems (DSS) [11].

**11. Kantoush, S.A., Sumi, T., Kojiri, T., Saber, M., Elshennawy, I., Awad, H., & Sefelnaser, A. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* JE-HydroNet: Modern Methodologies for the Management, Monitoring and Planning of Integrated Water Resources in the Nile Delta of Egypt [12].
* *Key Topic:* Integrated water resources management and flash flood prevention [12], [13].

**12. Kavetski, D., Evin, G., Clark, M.P., Thyer, M.A., Kuczera, G., Renard, B., Fenicia, F., & Tuteja, N. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Battling hydrological monsters: Insights into numerical approximations, data uncertainty and structural errors [14].
* *Key Topic:* Conceptual hydrology, model inference, numerical errors, and data uncertainty [14], [15].

**13. Koutsourelakis, I.G., & Memos, C.D. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Wave breaking and bottom friction over mildly sloping bed and bar in a new Boussinesq wave model [16].
* *Key Topic:* Wave breaking, bottom friction, and Boussinesq wave models [16].

**14. Ladson, A.R., Hillemacher, M.A., & Treadwell, S.A. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Lake Wellington Salinity: Investigation of Management Options [17].
* *Key Topic:* Estuarine processes and salinity management [17].

**15. Lee, H.S., Ding, F., Hendri, & Yamashita, T. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Natural hazards education using Regional Environment Simulator [18].
* *Key Topic:* Natural hazard education and meso-scale numerical modeling [18].

**16. Liu, Y., & Pender, G. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* A New Fast Inundation Model Using Support Vector Regression and Modified Linear Interpolation [19].
* *Key Topic:* Inundation modeling and support vector regression [19].

**17. Ooi, S.K., Sisomphon, P., Kurniawan, A., & Gerritsen, H. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Modeling of Sea Level and Current Anomalies in the Singapore Region [20].
* *Key Topic:* Tide-surge interaction, sea level and current anomalies [20], [21].

**18. Rong, Y., Wei, L., & Bai, L. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Extreme rainfall events in the sources of the Yangtzi and the Yellow Rivers [22].
* *Key Topic:* Rainfall extreme events and climate change impacts in river sources [22], [23].

**19. Shrivastava, G.S. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* University Education in Hydraulic Engineering: A view from the Caribbean Islands [24].
* *Key Topic:* Hydraulic engineering education, disasters, and coastal issues [24].

**20. St-Germain, P., Nistor, I., Palermo, D., & Townsend, R. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* SPH Modeling of Extreme Hydrodynamic Forces on Slender Structures [25].
* *Key Topic:* Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (SPH) modeling, tsunami forces, and dam-break dynamics [25].

**21. Uijttewaal, W.S.J. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Horizontal mixing in shallow flows [26].
* *Key Topic:* Shallow flows, mixing layers, secondary circulation, and compound channels [26], [27].

**22. Unknown Authors (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Invalidity of Classical Log-Law and Its Mechanism [28].
* *Key Topic:* Turbulent flow features, Reynolds shear stress, and velocity fluctuations [28]. *(Note: Primary authors were omitted in the source header, though related to the works of S.Q. Yang)* [28], [29].

**23. Yang, H.Y., Lin, B.L., & Zhou, J.J. (2011).**
* *Full Title:* Assessment of Morphological Properties of a Braided River Predicted by a Process-based Model [30].
* *Key Topic:* Braided river morphology and sediment transport modeling [30].
